data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T163938-90327348/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T163938-90327348/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T163938-90327348/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T163938-90327348/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T163938-90327348/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-a2212280">
    <title>agent: remove ResolveRoute preflight from work execution paths</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx execution paths still call ResolveRoute as a preflight even though Fizeau owns service routing for agent execution. That keeps DDx coupled to route-preview semantics during runtime execution and risks alternate behavior between status/debug surfaces and actual work execution.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:609-653 defines buildRoutePreflight and calls ResolveRoute before executing worker tasks.
- cli/internal/server/workers_route_preflight_test.go asserts this preflight behavior, so tests currently lock in the boundary violation.
- cmd routing lint currently checks command execution paths but does not cover server worker execution in cli/internal/server/workers.go.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove ResolveRoute preflight from worker execution paths. Keep ResolveRoute only on explicit status/debug/preview surfaces such as ddx agent route-status. Add lint/test coverage so non-status execution paths fail if they reintroduce ResolveRoute.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing route-status or provider/model inventory features.
- Changing Fizeau routing APIs.
- Changing final actual-route recording from Fizeau results.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. buildRoutePreflight and RoutePreflight wiring are deleted from WorkerManager.runWorker and related execution paths.
2. ResolveRoute remains available only in status/debug/preview surfaces, not in bead/work execution paths.
3. TestWorkerExecutionDoesNotCallResolveRouteForPinnedProfileOrModel uses a stub service whose ResolveRoute fails and proves worker execution still proceeds without calling it.
4. Existing route-preflight tests are removed or rewritten to assert the new boundary.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestRoutinglintNoResolveRouteInExecutionPaths|TestRoutinglintNonStatusFilesDoNotCallResolveRoute|TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute' -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run 'TestWorker.*ResolveRoute|TestWorker_NoPreflight|TestRunWorker' -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:server, area:routing, kind:cleanup, upstream-fizeau</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-dda48755" spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="940b59c846785ba63a461360535a78753fc124dc" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T163938-90327348"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Agent Service (consumer of ddx-agent contract)</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Removed the worker-side `ResolveRoute` preflight from server execution, replaced it with a server-side boundary test, and deleted the old preflight-only test file.

What changed:
- Removed `buildRoutePreflight` and its `RoutePreflight` wiring from [`cli/internal/server/workers.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a2212280-20260505T163938-90327348/cli/internal/server/workers.go)
- Deleted [`cli/internal/server/workers_route_preflight_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a2212280-20260505T163938-90327348/cli/internal/server/workers_route_preflight_test.go)
- Added [`cli/internal/server/workers_resolve_route_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a2212280-20260505T163938-90327348/cli/internal/server/workers_resolve_route_test.go) with:
  - `TestWorkerExecutionDoesNotCallResolveRouteForPinnedProfileOrModel`
  - `TestWorkerRoutinglintNoResolveRouteInExecutionPaths`

Verification:
- `go test ./cmd -run 'TestRoutinglintNoResolveRouteInExecutionPaths|TestRoutinglintNonStatusFilesDoNotCallResolveRoute|TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute' -count=1`
- `go test ./internal/server -run 'TestWorker.*ResolveRoute|TestWorker_NoPreflight|TestRunWorker' -count=1`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

Commit:
- `acbaba84` `fix: remove resolve-route preflight from worker execution [ddx-a2212280]`

One environment note: the repo’s pre-commit hook uses `-race`, so it only passes with `CGO_ENABLED=1` set.
